description_long: This object is a compact, rectangular radio receiver with a digital clock display on its front face, showing red LED numerals. The casing is primarily gray with some olive-green accents, and it features a perforated speaker grille on the top surface. Several tactile buttons are arranged on the top, likely for tuning, volume, and alarm functions. On one side, there is a cylindrical attachment, possibly a projector or sensor, integrated into the main body. The back and sides are smooth, with minimal ports or connectors visible, and the overall design is streamlined for tabletop use. The object appears to be made of molded plastic with electronic components inside, typical of consumer electronics found in urban environments.
description: A gray, rectangular radio receiver with a digital clock display, top-mounted buttons, and a perforated speaker grille, featuring a side cylindrical attachment.
description_view_0: Front view showing the digital clock display, control buttons on top, and the cylindrical side attachment.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the smooth side panel, part of the top control area, and a small port near the base.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the main body and the cylindrical attachment, with no visible controls or display.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the end of the cylindrical attachment and the rounded edge of the main body.
